title: pilea ciliaris var. rivoriae
intro: Pilea ciliaris var. rivoriae, also known as 'Alpine Pilea' or 'Rivoria Pilea', is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to the tropical forests of the Philippines. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Pilea ciliaris var. rivoriae include its ability to aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically harvested, dried, and then used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are believed to harness its medicinal properties and offer relief from various health issues.
